# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

```yaml
apiVersion: v1  # API version (v1) that the YAML configuration file describes, e.g., core or networking APIs you're using in Kubernetes Cluster/Environment  
kind: Service    # Specifies what kind of resource this is - a `Service` for service creation and management etc...  This could be anything defined by k8s API like Pod which defines an instance to run at cluster level, or ReplicaSet that sets up desired pod replication e.t.. the last one being our Service
metadata:       # Specifies how metadata is associated with this resource - for example name/namespace etc...   The fields are 'name', namespace and labels where applicable  Also tags can be used to identify different resources in terms of category or sub-category, as well tagging helps manage a complex topology scenario e.g., "app=myApp;env=prod"
    annotations: # Specifies metadata associated with this resource - for example deployment/pods etc...  You have these annotation like linkerd injector where you set the configuration details about how to apply proxy and authorization policy on Kubernetes services. This also can be used in other environments such as openshift, aks which provide different configurations
        links:       # Specifies metadata associated with this resource - for example deployment/pods etc...   Field is `links` where we are using linkerd injector to configure service mesh on K8S clusters.  This field contains details about how many replicas of each Pod should be created, which configuration settings and so forth
            app: myApp     # Namespace for the application that will use this Service (i e., microservices). It is assumed there are one namespace named 'myapp' in cluster   Also includes other parameters like name & env as it can differ across environments.  Example would be `linkerd inject --namespace=<NAMESPACE> | kubectl apply -f <SERVICE_MESH>`
            app: myApp     # Namespace for the application that will use this Service (i e., microservices). It is assumed there are one namespace named 'myapp' in cluster   Also includes other parameters like name & env as it can differ across environments.  Example would be `linkerd inject --namespace=<NAMESPACE> | kubectl apply -f <SERVICE
